version_date: April 2021 (published April 2021; supersedes SPP 3.6 Development Contributions for Infrastructure 2009)
scope_summary: State Planning Policy made under Part 3 of the Planning and Development Act 2005 setting the principles and requirements for levying infrastructure contributions from developers and landowners in new and established areas across Western Australia. It distinguishes Development Infrastructure (standard contributions imposed through subdivision/development conditions, Schedule 1) from Community Infrastructure (capped contributions levied through a Development Contribution Plan, Schedule 2), and prescribes DCP preparation, scheme incorporation, cost apportionment, indexation, credits, annual reporting, dispute resolution and closure requirements. It primarily affects subdivision and development of growth areas rather than the design assessment of individual dwellings.

key_numeric_standards.applies_to: land for public open space (local and district) at subdivision
local governments seeking contributions for capital cost of community infrastructure via a DCP

key_numeric_standards.topic: restricted public open space allowance for wetlands
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: wetland land credited as part of public open space where useable for recreation

key_numeric_standards.unit: years (up to 15 years in limited circumstances for community infrastructure)

key_numeric_standards.applies_to: review of SPP 3.6 including community infrastructure caps
landowner objecting to a cost contribution amount

cross_references.cited: Planning and Development (Local Planning Schemes) Regulations 2015
WAPC Operational Policy for School Site Planning

quality_flags: two-column PDF layout extracted with interleaved reading order in places (table of contents and side-by-side sections merge mid-sentence)

exact gazettal day not stated in extracted text - only 'April 2021' publication date is visible
minor OCR/typo artifacts retained from source (e.g. 'State of Western Australian', 'Clause 6.9.19' which does not exist - likely meaning 6.10.19)
